note_data.title: Programming scenarios practice
keyPoints: Binary system is used to process data in logic gates/transistors, to store data in registers, and to process data on the computer
Denary is base 10 using digits 0-9, Binary is base 2 using 0s and 1s, Hexadecimal is base 16 using digits 0-9 and alphabets A-F
Hexadecimal is used in colour codes, error codes and IP & MAC addresses because it is easily understandable, easier to debug, and takes less space on the screen
For binary to hexadecimal conversion, break binary into sets of 4 digits
Each shift left is equivalent to multiplying the binary number by 2^n, each shift right is equivalent to dividing the binary number by 2^n
Overflow error occurs when the value is greater than the maximum value of the register
Two's complement format is used to represent negative binary numbers where the leftmost bit is changed to a negative value
A character set is a list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer where each character and symbol has a unique value
Sampling is used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals to give an approximate representation of the sound wave

definitions.definition: A list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer
Used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals. This gives an approximate representation of the sound wave
The number of samples taken per second
The number of bits required per sample (i.e: bit depth)

howToAnswer.instruction: List the specific inputs, processes or outputs without explanation
Produce complete code with correct syntax, variable names and logical structure
Give the method/process and explain how it works using technical terms
Provide specific test data with expected outcomes for normal, boundary and erroneous cases

modelAnswer.question: A school in Lahore needs a program to calculate student exam grades. Students enter their marks for 3 subjects (0-100). The program calculates the average and displays the grade: A (80+), B (70-79), C (60-69), D (50-59), F (below 50). Identify the inputs, processes and outputs for this scenario.
modelAnswer.answer:
INPUTS: Three subject marks (INTEGER values 0-100)
PROCESSES: Validate marks are in range 0-100, Calculate average = (mark1 + mark2 + mark3) / 3, Determine grade using IF statements based on average
OUTPUTS: Average mark (REAL), Grade letter (STRING), Appropriate message displaying results
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct identification of three subject marks as inputs

commonMistakes: Forgetting that overflow error occurs when carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size
Losing leftmost 1 bits while shifting left or rightmost 1 bits while shifting right will cause errors
Not remembering that in two's complement format the leftmost bit determines the sign of the number
Confusing the process of converting negative denary to binary - must write positive binary first, then invert all values, then add 1
examinerTips: Remember that hexadecimal has fewer digits for the same value compared to binary, making it easier to work with
When converting binary to hexadecimal, always break into sets of 4 digits from right to left
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: IPO diagram showing three columns: Inputs (user data entry), Processes (calculations and decisions), Outputs (results displayed), with arrows showing data flow from left to right
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to write pseudocode for a library book borrowing system with validation
O/N 2022 P2 Q5 (8 marks) tested a stock management scenario where candidates had to implement search and update functions with file handling
M/J 2022 P2 Q3 (5 marks) required students to design a simple calculator program with error handling for division by zero
O/N 2023 P2 Q6 (7 marks) focused on a student grade processing system with arrays and grade boundary calculations
M/J 2021 P2 Q4 (6 marks) tested a parking meter simulation requiring time calculations and payment validation

workedExample.question: A shop wants to calculate customer bills. Write pseudocode that: inputs item prices until -1 is entered, applies 10% discount if total > $100, adds 8% tax, then outputs final amount. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Initialize total = 0 and set up input loop with sentinel value -1
Step 2: Use REPEAT-UNTIL loop to input prices and accumulate total
Step 3: Check if total > 100, if true then apply 10% discount using total = total * 0.9
Step 4: Calculate tax using total = total * 1.08
Step 5: Output the final amount with appropriate message
workedExample.answer:
total ← 0
REPEAT
  INPUT price
  IF price ≠ -1 THEN
    total ← total + price
  ENDIF
UNTIL price = -1
IF total > 100 THEN
  total ← total * 0.9
ENDIF
total ← total * 1.08
OUTPUT "Final amount: $", total
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct loop structure, 1 mark for sentinel value handling, 1 mark for accumulating total, 1 mark for discount condition, 1 mark for tax calculation, 1 mark for appropriate output

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to include proper input validation' — always check for invalid inputs like negative numbers or empty strings when solving practical scenarios
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often forgot to initialize variables at the start of their programs' — always declare and initialize all variables before use, especially counters and totals

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates frequently mixed up assignment and comparison operators' — remember to use ← or = for assignment and = or == for comparison depending on the pseudocode style required
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a program flow diagram showing: START oval, INPUT parallelogram for data entry, PROCESS rectangles for calculations/operations, DECISION diamonds for IF statements and loop conditions with YES/NO paths, OUTPUT parallelogram for displaying results, and END oval. Arrows must show clear flow direction, and decision points must have clearly labeled paths for different conditions.
note_data.quickRecap: Binary is the fundamental number system used by computers for processing and storing data. Number system conversions between denary, binary and hexadecimal follow specific methods including breaking binary into sets of 4 for hexadecimal conversion. Binary operations include logical shifts for multiplication/division and two's complement for representing negative numbers. Text and sound are converted to binary through character sets and sampling respectively.
